title: salix lapponum subsp. marrubiifolia
intro: Salix lapponum subsp. marrubiifolia, also known as the Balsam Willow, is a subspecies of the Salix genus, characterized by its distinctive leaves and medicinal properties. The plant is used to treat various health conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling in joints and wounds. The health benefits of Salix lapponum subsp. marrubiifolia include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, salves, and infusions, often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
